resolvedQuestions: When should sk-design auto-pair interface, foundations and audit?
What context must be loaded before visual choices?
How should contrast discipline be encoded before implementation?
What audit artifacts must be loaded before release claims?
What must be passed to dispatched sub-agents and small models?
How should MiniMax-M3 prompts differ from frontier-model prompts?
What hard gates prevent repeat misses?
How should fan-out outputs be verified and adopted?

keyFindings.title: UI build work needs a context bundle, not interface-only routing
keyFindings.summary: The hub says generic design prompts default to interface, but interface itself requires the shared register, dials, mechanical pre-flight, and quality floor. Any build or redesign task should load interface plus register plus foundations contrast and audit pre-flight context.

keyFindings.title: The Brand-vs-Product register must be a hard first-step gate
keyFindings.summary: The shared register says skipping it is the common cause of generic drift and sets six downstream dials. Interface, foundations and audit each load it first, so missing it is a workflow failure.

keyFindings.title: Contrast must be checked as token-pair inventory before build handoff
keyFindings.summary: Foundations owns contrast work; OKLCH repair starts from actual foreground/background pairs, and interface quality says WCAG AA must be verified, not eyeballed.

keyFindings.title: Audit must use the pre-flight and evidence contracts, not ad-hoc critique
keyFindings.summary: The pre-flight card is a binary ship/fix gate; the audit contract is a severity, evidence and score contract. Both prevent release claims from unsupported visual judgment.

keyFindings.title: Sub-agents need a sk-design context manifest with required reads and proof fields
keyFindings.summary: Dispatch prompts must include the spec folder, allowed write scope, loaded references, output format, and verification. Otherwise delegated agents cannot satisfy Gate 3 or prove they loaded the right mode context.

keyFindings.title: MiniMax-M3 must receive the profiled TIDD-EC dense scaffold
keyFindings.summary: MiniMax-M3 is an explicit exception to generic prompt defaults; the model profile says TIDD-EC plus dense pre-planning and explicit Do/Don't sections are the mechanism that curbs drift.

keyFindings.title: Adopt-if-better needs merge attribution plus promotion gates
keyFindings.summary: Fan-out can deduplicate and preserve lineage attribution, but it does not by itself prove a recommendation is better. Canonical adoption should require score, benchmark or deterministic checks, boundary compliance, and operator approval.

ruledOutDirections: Automatic adoption from a single lineage recommendation
